title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After receiving permission from the Earl of Carringham, Anthony Kidd accompanies Chief Constable Bartlett to the room where the murder occurred. Anthony explains that the gunshot sounded as if it came from the corner room, which he initially attributed to a poacher. Bartlett reveals a discrepancy: while Anthony claims all three windows were locked from the inside, the middle window was found unlatched this morning. Anthony suggests this implies either an inhabitant of the house opened the window to frame an outsider or that he is being falsely accused of lying. Bartlett admits he suspected Anthony due to obvious footprints, but implies he is considering the possibility of an inside job.

Bartlett presents a piece of paper found under the body featuring a pattern associated with the Red Hand Brotherhood. Anthony identifies the pattern but argues that the organization is unlikely to commit murder or blend in as guests. He concludes that the open window and footprints are part of a scheme, though he denies being an informant for the group. Bartlett then leads Anthony to a sitting room to view the corpse. Anthony recognizes the deceased as Prince Michael O'Paulverich, who had previously introduced himself under the alias "Holmes," a representative from Packardson Haggikin Publishers.

Meanwhile, in the Blue Room, Mr. Roxburgh introduces Chief Constable Bartlett to Herman Eschelbacher, a wealthy financier. Roxburgh and Eschelbacher discuss the murder privately, questioning Anthony's innocence. Bartlett states he will investigate Anthony's background in South America but remains cautious. He dismisses the theory that the Red Hand Brotherhood is responsible, asserting that the printed paper was planted as a distraction. Bartlett maintains that the investigation must first determine the motive and identify who stands to gain most from Michael's death.
